reviews.review_description:
What a fantastic game. You really get lost in the experience.

I will say it does feel more like a $12.99 game as it is a 2D game that could have easily been done on the Kinect or a similar older game console system with motion detection (probably more like $15.99 since they allow custom music). I got it for $19.99. The app says more official songs are coming, so I won't hold it against them for not having more songs in the game.

With the custom music, it would be nice to filter by length and genre. The game play only feels right with classical music (I'm not even a fan of classical music but I absolutely love it in this game) and anything over the 6 minutes or under 1 minute isn't fun for me. I can't get a preview of the music in the menu screen until I've played it which defeats the purpose of a preview. Also, it seems to only preview the last three custom songs I've played.

The hand tracking is a little bad even with great lighting, which isn't an issue until you try the more extreme custom songs.  But I don't know if this is a hardware or software issue as all my other rhythm games use my remote controls.

I'm just hoping they're still working on this game to do some more tweaks and to add more songs. Despite all my nitpicking above, it really is a fantastic game.

Symphoni is a bit of a mixed bag. On one hand, the visuals are quite impressive—the Mixed Reality portals in your walls look magical, and it’s a solid showcase for the Quest 3’s capabilities. The classical music is grand and sounds great in VR.
On the other hand, the game feels hollow because there is no meaningful progression or story. You just wave your hands to the rhythm, level after level, but it quickly becomes repetitive. There’s no narrative to keep you engaged or make you feel like you’re achieving something. It gets boring after the first 30 minutes.
It’s definitely not the worst app in the store—it’s polished and aesthetically pleasing. However, as a full-fledged game, it’s lacking. Good for a quick zen session, but don't expect an exciting adventure or long-term engagement.

This was the first game I had decided to get - months before getting my first-ever MQ3 - and the first I ever played in my new VR journey. 

Captivating in every way. The graphics are exceptional, and the music sweeps me up inside itself every time. My whole body gets engaged while I conduct the music. 

I keep recommending this beautiful experience to everyone I can! It’s my go-to when I need to escape. 

I would love new pieces - any chance we’ll see updates anytime soon?

reviews.review_title: A new game changer for VR rhythm games
reviews.review_description: Symphoni breaks away from the 1st generation rhythm game style, which has become overly familiar and can feel stale. By fully utilizing MR (Mixed Reality) and hand tracking, key features of 2nd generation rhythm games, you no longer need to unnecessarily hold cumbersome controllers to play the rhythm. The clean and simple UI, ethereal effects, and refreshing, spreading sound effects harmonize beautifully with the game's classical music-based atmosphere. I'm particularly pleased with the custom mode added in this update, which greatly enhances the game's content. This isn't a game where you act as a maestro directing musicians. Instead, it's like a Harry Potter game where you become a wizard, using a wand to perform music directly. The only drawback is that it currently only supports English. It would be wonderful if multi-language support could be added for users from various countries.

I really wanted this game to be great. I love classical music. I started with beat saber and moved mainly to synthriders because the synthriders just works. I tried symphoni and the idea of conducting in vr is brilliant. I’ve played the piano since age 3 and am in the middle of a conducting course. 
The movements in this game don’t have a lot to do with conducting. The hand tracking in horrendous. You kill yourself to throw your hands up and it decides that you didn’t cut the arc. It ruins your spirit and makes you feel cheated. Then they have combined movements that are so unnatural that without being just memorized you’ll never do them. It’s also very hard to see this arcs come at you. It’s not like synthriders or beatsaber where you get a stream and you can plan ahead. Here everything is a surprise. There is so much place for a vr-conducting game. Right now this is a disappointment. Sadly…

I was one of the testers for this game, so factor that in, but I absolutely adore this game. It's a music rhythm game with a great mix of classical music. The difficulties vary from casual, where you can just enjoy the magical environments and atmosphere, to the harder ones where it's serious challenge. Personally I prefer the more casual experience of easy or medium, but this has something for everyone. I can't recommend this game enough. 👍

The game is a lot of fun even if the graphics are very simple and abstract, and the movements of the hands that have to be done here when conducting have virtually nothing to do with actual movements when conducting. The additional content thanks to Composer makes the game more varied, a few very good additional pieces already exist (in addition to some crusy user creations)
Beautifully and elegantly done. Very hypnotic and draws you in with the music. Can definitely recommend this one.
I have enjoyed it a lot

reviews.review_title: Great for beginners and hardcore fans!
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE 25/04/25

XXXXXXXX

Now has custom songs that you can download all from the headset (no need for sidequest)
The game just when from fantastic to.........OMG.
Ive been having a blast with some of the songs the community has made already.

This title has permanent fixture on my headset now!!
Ill be hopping back in regularly to see what new tracks the community has made.

XXXXXXXXX

This game will have you playing to a collection of 16 classical tunes (at launch) in both MR and VR modes.

With both hand tracking and controller modes you can really take advantage of all the features the Quest 3/3s has to offer.

Whether your a casual music game player or the more experience player there is a difficulty (3 difficulties) to suit everyone as compete for high scores.

Whist the package may seem modest (but fairly priced) all the songs are excellently charted and balanced in a nice gradual curve to help you master the game.

But the best is yet to come with the developers already set the motions on building a community up........eg custom songs!!

I have a feeling that this title will grow from strength to strength as the community grows with the title.

reviews.review_title: Amazing Twist on the Rhythm Game Genre
reviews.review_description: As a seasoned Beat Saber player, I was sceptical of the idea of a rhythm game centered around classical music, but now that I've played it, I could not imagine this game any other way. The art style, game play and music all complement each other perfectly. Great concert, great game, genuinely fun to play.

I’ve been lucky enough to have experienced this game from the early alpha demos through the final iterations, and it has been a beautiful experience from start to finish. I’m a long-time lover of music and rhythm games and this one is as much a new way of playing music as playing with music. The hand-tracking gameplay feels natural, with the music coming from my fingertips, and the mixed-reality environment bringing it into my space. I’m so excited for the future of this game, from mastering the campaign, exploring and eventually creating more songs, and 100%ing the achievements. Thank you Symphoni for making a new, meaningful way of engaging with music and VR!

Wow! 

Incredible experiences in VR don't come along often as a tester and with my mountain of games I have played it gets a bit... repetitive sometimes.

Boundaries aren't often pushed and genres are pretty much locked in.

I honestly didn't expect Symphoni to be anything but ok as a rhythm game - same old, same old right?

Nope!

I AM A CONDUCTOR!!!

I wave around my conductors wand pointing and swiping and swishing along to beautiful classical music (or anything I want if I use the composer app) and visually the screen lights up with incredible displays that move with my hand movements! 

Breathtaking!

There are a solid amount of songs for the price, with 4 distinct themes and many recognisable classics from Four Seasons to Toccata & Fugue.

You can also play in VR or MR, with controllers or hand tracking. I highly recommend trying every combination!

Replayability is high on my list when it comes to choosing what to play in VR. Symphoni has achievements, 3 distinct difficulty modes and the aforementioned song editor - the Composer.

Trust me you will be returning to this game again and again, whether it be to beat that high score, just relax and enjoy a favourite opera or test out something from your Spotify playlist!!!

I don't say this often in reviews but this is a must buy! It is polished, well designed and a unique immersive experience that nothing else can match!

Now back to conducting. Sssh!

❤️

I tested this game before release, so I can say I’ve played right through most levels and it brilliant. 

Symphoni UI is essentially a big ring in front of you and you are the wizard maestro of sorts and have to strike the coloured arch’s (notes) that come at you at the right time for maximum points. 

You have varied levels to allow all levels of play! But you can get so immersed into this game it’s unreal! 

You play through and unlock achievements and new cool wands as you play through the game.

Some archs (notes) switch over some you need to hit with both hands! 

This offers controller or hand tracking and also a cool Mixed Reality Mode! 

Rhythm games fans will dig this I absolutely adore it!

Symphoni offers a unique twist on the rhythm game genre by blending the experience of conducting with the art of casting spells across sixteen classical pieces. Its minimalist visual design is effective in both virtual and mixed reality settings. Personally, I find the virtual reality mode to be the most visually captivating, truly capturing the sense of synesthesia that studio founder Imgram Mao aimed to achieve. Currently, the game features only classical music tracks, but there’s a track editor available, and the team is encouraging players to create their own custom tracks. They also plan to introduce more free tracks through updates in the future. As with any rhythm game, its success may heavily rely on the creativity of the modding community.

reviews.review_title: Beautiful addition to the rhythm genre!
reviews.review_description: I first tried this game at GDC last year and while it was a blast then, it's simply SO GOOD now! It's one thing to listen to your favorite classical music, and an entirely otherworldly experience to actually conduct your favorite songs with mixed reality environments. GOOSEBUMPS! Hand tracking makes it feel very natural too. Highly recommend!

I am unable to truly enjoy this game. Not because of the game but because of my severe color blindness. I know to most people the two colors in this game may look very different, but to me they do not. If there was a way you could have an option where the two colors are very different from each other like a very bright red and very dark blue then that would make it possible for me to enjoy this game because currently I am unable. 😞 Thanks. If this kind of update is not possible please let me know so I can ask for a refund. No fault of the game, just my sucky eyes. Thank you.

developer_response.body:
I am terribly sorry we haven’t provided these accessibility options at launch. This will definitely be in our roadmap this year so we can make the game more accessible for everyone!

Come join our discord community: https://discord.gg/symphoni 
We would love for you to test this feature out when we begin to incorporate these accessibility options.

reviews.review_description:
I have been playing this since it's launch yesterday and I must say what a joy it is. 
It makes you feel like you are actually conducting a piece of your favourite classical music. 
The colours and vibrancy are amazing and I have found it to be a wonderful immersive experience.
I can't wait to see what the developers have in store for us in the future.
It's a definite 10 out of 10 from me.

I finally found something that perfectly combines my love for the Harry Potter franchise and Tchaikovsky, and it's awesome. The game is beautiful and felt healing to play - it was immensely satisfying to release the dramatic maestro within myself. Would recommend the game to anyone who wants to stop sweating to Beat Saber!

I didn't feel like I was conducting at all,  moving my hands a little to hit the lights on time. 

If your looking for a good conducting game I recommend maestro

Impressed by symphonis take on VR rhythm games! It has new ways of interacting with the music that I haven't seen before and the coordination required with the different interactions was challenging in a good way that made my brain think differently compared to when I play beatsaber. Beautiful graphics and UI as well, excited to see where this game goes!!

After hearing the news that this game landed at SXSW I played it right away in my own living room. I’ve played a ton of rhythm games in VR, but Symphoni, This is on a whole other level. Instead of just hitting notes, you’re actually conducting a freaking orchestra with your hands. And with the MR mode, it’s like my entire room transforms into this epic, spellbinding concert hall.

The music SLAPS
We’re talking Tchaikovsky, Beethoven, Bach—absolute legends. But Swan was easily my favorite. 

Kudos to the creators. Amazing job.

reviews.review_title: If you liked Maestro, give this a spin
reviews.review_description:
I.. really.. really love the vibes of this game. The use of MR -> VR modes while playing is incredible, top marks for execution.

Solid collection of classical tracks, lost myself to a bunch of them!!

however... easy mode felt wayyyyy too easy, and hard *too hard* .. I can't help but think I've been spoilt by the "Normal" difficulty on Beat Saber :D For sure "Hard" mode in Symphoni feels like it could be Expert+

Looking forward though to getting a habit going and conquering a Hard level soon!

I love this game, super addicting gameplay but also relaxing and in my case helps me focus.  So many good songs, gameplay and fun!  Clear buy from my side 5 stars!!!! :) I made a short gameplay video on YT on the Beard in VR channel if you search for it.  Great game, awesome job Dev team !

Being a newbie in VR game, symphoni is the first hand-controlled game on Quest 3 that I do not need my controllers. The classical music collections are great, the tracking and playing experience is great, and visual effect is great. I expect myself to play many hours on Symphoni.

This is a magical MR music game where you use your hands to act like a musical conductor while moving your hands to the rhythm of the songs.  The music is peaceful and relaxing.  It gives you several levels of difficulty to try top the leader boards.  It is almost psychedelic seeing the visuals swarm around you.  If you love music games this is much different than any other you have experienced.  Musical people will definitely not be disappointed in this game!

reviews.review_title: game is fun, but there are some problems
reviews.review_description:
1. Right meta button error frequently. There were a few times I had to turn off the meta quest3 itself because it didn't work when I tried to shut down the game.

2. I hope the position is fixed. When I play the game, it's rearranged to its current position, which is quite inconvenient. There are times when I move a little back when I play a fast song, but it's inconvenient because it's rearranged every time.

3. This game is having trouble with the ranking system. I just test-played Swan Lake Easy mod and got 100% perfect. There were 9 people who were perfect other than me, So it should be 10th, but weirdly it's 6th. I've tried perfect some other songs with Easy mod and most of them are ranked weirdly.

Please correct these problems

developer_response.body: Thank you for the notes! We will make sure we can address them in future updates :)

reviews.review_description: The game is a very immersive experience with amazing visuals, and the controls are really easy to get the hang of and feel super natural. I spent a whole evening trying to beat every single level! 10/10, can't wait for more songs to drop

Symphoni has quickly become one of my daily go-to apps among the 300+ I’ve purchased since 2018 (definitely my favorite app to relax) I’m incredibly grateful for how Symphoni has helped my mental health & greatly improved my quality of life. The innovative portal concept is executed so elegantly & the haptic feedback is gentle yet precise. The gameplay is addicting and the menu & display are clean, stunning & so darn lovely. It all beautifully comes together along with mesmerizing visuals & curated classical masterpieces, featuring composers like Beethoven, Tchaikovsky, Vivaldi, Brahms, etc to create a serene, dreamlike ambiance. You can switch between VR & Mixed Reality which is awesome! Seeing fog drifting through my room really enhances the immersion & overall experience. Symphoni doesn’t explicitly advertise a research-based approach, but its design elements - immersive visuals, soothing music, and interactive engagement - mirror techniques found in apps rooted in neuroscience & psychology that I've used to promote relaxation (Liminal, etc). Symphoni has become my daily ritual, a sanctuary of peace, music & movement. A real magical experience. Music & meditation apps have been shown to reduce stress hormones & promote relaxation - Symphoni delivers that same calming effect & peace so beautifully. The seamless fusion of artistic beauty, intuitive interaction, and auditory excellence makes this one of the most enchanting & truly delightful experiences I've had on the Quest. I am not surprised at all that the first 60+ users that have rated this app have given this app a 5-Star rating average! To come out of the gate (or portal) that strong is very impressive & rare for a Quest game, a sign of a true masterpiece - and the devs are promising more to come! I deeply appreciate the care &: creativity the developers put behind this app & I'm extremely grateful that Symphoni is an essential part of my relaxation routine & I will cherish it for a long time to come 🙏

SO much fun - and fantastic music selection - you can tell the developers put so much attention to detail in this game and it makes everything so immersive! From the mixed reality environments to the wands and VFX it is all so captivating and really immerses you into music in a way that nothing else does

This game is a work of art in the VR/MR world.

It never fails to provide a fun and exciting experience with the variety of different classical songs, and each one provides a challenge in its own unique way.

Whether you’re a beat game enthusiast or just love classical music, I strongly recommend Symphoni for its premium experience. Definitely worth it!
Smooth controls, nice visuals, great selection of classical music pieces. Fun to play, easy to learn hard to master. Symphoni + Beat Saber + Synth Riders = perfect music combo. Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: A Musical Masterpiece in Mixed Reality
reviews.review_description: It's a fantastic blend of virtual and mixed reality. The vanishing environments and passthrough integration create a dynamic transition between fully immersive and real-world gameplay. Hand tracking and haptic feedback feel precise, making interactions with the batons satisfying. Visually, the game is stunning—its art direction evokes elegant digital artwork, with rich, atmospheric environments. As difficulty increases, the rhythmic challenge keeps players engaged, reinforcing a narrative of Virtuoso mastery. Beyond entertainment, it transforms classical music into an interactive journey. A brilliant fusion of art, music, and tech. Highly recommended as an avant-garde mixed-reality music experience.
It's really beautifull game, the concept magical realm mixed reality has great visual. The hand tracking work like magic. Make feels like conductor. 16 song list its perfect

reviews.review_title: Fantastic game that brings something new
reviews.review_description: Great game, fun songs. I was a tester but you've gotta give it a try, the devs worked incredibly hard and its a brand new thing for VR rhythm games.

reviews.review_title: One of the best games I've played
reviews.review_description: This game takes you to another world. Time just disappears. It's hard to stop playing.
16 classic songs, simple gameplay, and minimalistic graphics—but incredibly addictive and challenging! The game works seamlessly with both hand tracking and controllers.

I just did 2 songs! 

This is AMAZING! 
So relaxing, so beautiful! 
I cant wait for more!!
Love it love it love it. Playing around like a big kid. Worth every penny!

Stumbling across Symphoni was such a pleasant surprise.  I was totally not expecting to be running for headphones and charging my headset up again to play more (that was a first)!  I love the use of mixed reality and hand tracking, it really gives an organic feel to everything.  Classical music is fantastic, but I can only imagine what this game would be if it had some synthy music in it.  Convert my room into a classy mystical forest and then a retro neon city?  Yes please!  

If you like a game that's candy for the eyes and ears, definitely give Symphoni a try!

I hadn’t seen a game in this genre tackle classical music before, and having the movements mimic conducting is a fun premise. It’s very satisfying when you get into the flow and everything glides together. Looking forward to more songs!

This game is actually kinda amazing. I came here, expecting a beat saber rip off, and holy cow, it's definitely not. The graphics are insane. The effects are insane. The haptics are also pretty good. I love how intuitive everything feels, and unlike beat saber, the controls and movement feel so much better. The only thing I can really complain about is that they don't have modern songs, but whatever. The current songs they have are good enough. This game is genuinely gorgeous and fun to play.

reviews.review_title: A highly artistic and fun rhythm game
reviews.review_description:
This VR rhythm game is incredibly innovative and fun. The artistic presentation and conductor-like controls make me feel deeply immersed in the music, maximizing the enjoyment. The difficulty balance is also well thought out, making it accessible to both beginners and advanced players alike.

One feature I particularly loved is the support for hand tracking. Being able to play comfortably without a controller adds to the overall convenience and immersion. I also really enjoyed how the background changes when achieving certain combos—it adds to the excitement.

This game truly maximizes the strengths of XR technology. I look forward to seeing more songs added in the future!

reviews.review_title: Really unique, makes the most of MR!
reviews.review_description: I have always wanted to conduct, and this game really makes that dream come true! If youre looking for a new rhythm game, something that will make you feel more connected to the music, or just fun gameplay, check this one out! There are a bunch of songs already available at launch, but I am just as excited to see what other ones will come available

I always wanted to play an arcade style rhythm game at home, and this game brings that to life with wonderful classical music as if you were managing an orchestra. Super responsive and customizable! As a rhythm game fan this is a wonderful game to both chill and also enjoy some very creative patterns on the harder difficulties 🥰

This game is a really cool way to enjoy classical music. I like how it lets you interact with the music instead of just listening—feels fun and different compare to other rhythm games. I wish the music genre can expand beyond classical in the future. Nice work.
I’ve had some issues with it just not realizing I hit a note thing so it just counts it as a zero, but other than that minor issue it’s great fun
Immersed in the world of music, the game's art is super amazing. The feeling of being surrounded by classical music is truly astonishing. It feels as if one's entire being has been purified.
I had so much fun with this! Really brilliantly brings together the creativity of music with the expression of movement. Truly a one of a kind experience!
One of the best MR games on the store, period. If you like Maestro or Beat Saber you will love this.

This is what I wanted to play!
Just great and worth play!!!
kind of like beat saber but with hand tracking. good range of difficulties.
